feedback.text: I would like this act to protect ownership of digital media. Digital items that are purchased should be yours to keep (download, alter, ...), or if it is you are buying a licence should be explicitly indicated that you do not own it. Specifis: 1. Games When games were mostly on physical media, there was no actual worry about this, nowadays some games are digital only and this trend is only growing. Digital games are a mess, even some games that should not require internet connection require it. Games are taken down and deleted from game libraries, as well as on user devices (example: The Crew by Ubisoft). Ubisoft even has a clause in their eula, that if they change the eula and you do not agree with new version of eula, you must delete items you have purchased. That is just crazy to be allowed. 2. Ebooks, comics and music The issues here are totally different, these are locked up with DRM so hard, it is not usable. I would prefer this media, to be without DRM, so I can use it with the apps or software I want, not the ones DRM is locked to. DRM free in this cases is just a rarity and never for popular media. If you purchased it you should be able to download it and use it in any software or app. thank you.
